en.title: Best Noise Cancelling Headphones 2026
en.description: Tracks the top over-ear noise cancelling headphones for commuters, travelers, and work-from-home users.

zh-tw.title: Best Noise Cancelling Headphones 2026
zh-tw.description: Tracks the top over-ear noise cancelling headphones for commuters, travelers, and work-from-home users.

en.description: Sony's flagship ANC headphones with the QN3 chip, offering class-leading noise cancellation, LDAC support, and a redesigned foldable frame.
Bose's premium flagship with ActiveSense ANC that dynamically adjusts to surroundings and Immersive Audio spatial sound mode.
Audiophile-grade sound with adaptive ANC and an industry-leading 60-hour battery life at a mid-range price.
Apple's premium over-ear headphones with H2 chip, Adaptive Audio, lossless audio via USB-C, and deep Apple ecosystem integration.
Budget ANC headphones with rare LDAC support and 50-hour battery that punch far above their price point.
Sony's lightest ANC headphone at 192g, offering solid noise cancellation and 35-hour battery for budget-conscious buyers.
Luxury over-ear headphones with Carbon Cone drivers and Nappa leather construction, targeting audiophiles who demand premium materials.
A beloved Bose classic with TriPort acoustic architecture and exceptional comfort, now available at a discounted legacy price.
Enterprise-focused headset with a 10-microphone array, best-in-class call quality, and 37-hour battery for all-day meetings.
Teams-certified headset with a 13-level ANC dial and dedicated Teams button, built for Microsoft 365 enterprise workflows.

en.commentary:
The noise-cancelling headphone market in Q1 2026 is headlined by the Sony WH-1000XM6, which launches with the new QN3 chip—seven times faster than its predecessor—to claim the top spot across major review outlets including RTINGS and Wirecutter. Its combination of class-leading ANC performance, excellent sound via LDAC, a redesigned foldable frame, and a competitive street price around $319–$399 makes it the definitive best-overall pick.

Bose's QuietComfort Ultra earns a close second thanks to ActiveSense technology that dynamically adapts noise cancellation in real time; some reviewers give it a narrow edge over the XM6 for sudden noise spikes. The Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless secures third place with exceptional audiophile-grade sound and a remarkable 60-hour ANC battery life—the longest of any headphone in this ranking—at a street price often below $280.

Apple's AirPods Max ranks fourth; sound quality is outstanding for Apple ecosystem users, but the 20-hour battery and $549 price limit broader appeal. The Soundcore Space Q45 is the value standout at fifth: LDAC support, adaptive ANC, and 50-hour battery at roughly $100 represent extraordinary value. The Sony WH-CH720N rounds out the budget tier at sixth—at under $150, it remains Sony's lightest ANC design at 192g with solid performance.

The Bowers & Wilkins Px8 in seventh boasts the best sound quality score (9.5 via Carbon Cone drivers), but its $699 price and merely-good ANC limit its overall standing. The Bose QC45 remains a comfortable legacy option at a discounted price, while the Jabra Evolve2 85 and Microsoft Surface Headphones 2+ are specialized enterprise tools optimized for call environments rather than general consumer listening.
highlights: Sony WH-1000XM6 debuts as #1 with the QN3 chip delivering the strongest ANC performance of any consumer headphone in 2026
Sennheiser Momentum 4 earns third place with an unmatched 60-hour ANC battery life at a mid-range price
Soundcore Space Q45 at ~$100 is RTINGS' top budget ANC pick, with rare LDAC support punching well above its class
Bowers & Wilkins Px8 scores highest for sound quality (9.5) but ranks 7th due to its $699 price and average ANC performance
